description_long: This is a large, round bowl with a wide, open mouth and a deep basin, supported by a thick, flared pedestal base. The bowl appears to be made from a single piece of dark, matte ceramic or clay, with a slightly uneven, hand-crafted texture visible across its surface. The rim is thick and smoothly rounded, while the body gently curves outward before tapering into the stem. The pedestal base is broad and stable, providing a solid foundation for the bowl. There are no visible handles, spouts, or decorative elements, giving the object a utilitarian and somewhat rustic appearance. The interior is empty and matches the exterior in color and finish.
description: A large, round, dark ceramic bowl with a wide mouth and a flared pedestal base, featuring a matte, hand-crafted finish and no visible decoration.
description_view_0: The bowl is seen from a slightly leftward angle, showing the wide opening, thick rim, and the full curve of the basin leading to the flared base.
description_view_1: This view highlights the right side of the bowl, emphasizing the smooth, rounded body and the broad, stable pedestal.
description_view_2: The front of the bowl is visible, with the open mouth and the symmetrical, gently sloping sides clearly shown.
description_view_3: The bowl is viewed from a slightly rightward angle, displaying the continuous curve from rim to base and the even, matte surface.
attribute_location: kitchen or outdoor communal dining area
attribute_context_of_use: serving or holding food, such as fruits, grains, or communal dishes

license_info.creator_display_name: The Royal Museums of Art and History
